system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Evolution WhatsApp AI Agent

Deploy a multi-tenant WhatsApp AI support and sales agent. Each client links their WhatsApp Business number → Evolution API instance → webhook to a FastAPI service → AI response back via Evolution.

## Architecture (v3.0.1 — May 2026)

```
Client WhatsApp Business → Evolution API Instance
    → POST /webhook/{instance} (incoming message)
        → FastAPI router → client system prompt + conversation history
            → LightRAG query (entity-aware memory, port 9623)  ← also has PAST CHAT HISTORY context
            → Evolution DB query (past messages for this contact)  ← NEW in v2.1.0
                → AI (DeepSeek with tool-calling → Groq fallback)
                    → Function execution (products, hours, policies)
                    → Second-pass AI with tool results
                        → Evolution API /message/sendText → Customer

                    ← Meanwhile, LightRAG gets batch-imported with past chats
                       via POST /clients/{instance}/import

                    ← Postgres persists clients in "HermesClient" table
                       per-client personality via PATCH /clients/{inst}/personality
```

### Hermes Integration

The WhatsApp agent runs alongside Hermes Agent but NOT as a gateway adapter. In Hermes v0.11.0, `whatsapp_provider: evolution` is a no-op — the gateway only supports the built-in Baileys bridge. Evolution runs as a standalone service:

 | Component | Role | Port |
|-----------|------|------|
| **Evolution API** | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — message send/receive | 8080 |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web interface at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager | (browser) |
| **FastAPI (this service)** | Webhook receiver + client management + history import | 8000 |
| **Evolution Postgres** | Message store (43k+ messages, 409 contacts) — accessed directly | 5432 |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware long-term memory per contact | 9623 |
| **Hermes-lcm plugin** | Lossless DAG context for long conversations | (plugin, no port) |
| **Skill Factory** | Auto-generates improved sales/support scripts | (plugin, reload on /reset) |
| **Traefik** | Public ingress at whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud | 443 |

### History Import (New in v2.1.0)

Evolution API v2.3.7 has **no REST endpoint** to retrieve past messages. All `/chat/findMessages`, `/chat/getMessages`, `/message/list` endpoints return 404. The only way to access stored messages is **direct Postgres connection** to Evolution's database.

The agent now does this:
1. Connects to `postgres:5432/evolution` via asyncpg
2. Queries the `Message` table (43,741 messages, back to June 2024) using `"remoteJid"` from the `key` JSONB column
3. Before each AI response, fetches recent past messages for that contact from Evolution's DB
4. Can batch-import entire chat histories into LightRAG via `POST /clients/{instance}/import`

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for full table structure and query patterns.

### LightRAG Integration (Deployed)

LightRAG runs as a Docker container on port 9623 with its own restart policy and volume persistence. Before each AI response, it's queried for entity-aware context:

```python
rag_url = "http://lightrag:9623"  # Docker network; also reachable as localhost outside container
rag_payload = {
    "query": "what does the customer know about? what products have they asked about?",
    "mode": "hybrid"  # local + global graph search
}
rag_response = requests.post(f"{rag_url}/query", json=rag_payload)
```

**What changed this round (May 5):** Migrated from raw systemd process to Docker container with restart-always policy. LightRAG now survives host reboots. Uses DeepSeek (`deepseek-chat`) as LLM backend (via `LLM_BINDING_HOST` env) and `bge-m3` for 1024-dimensional embeddings via Ollama.

**Ollama runs on host** bound to `0.0.0.0:11434` (not `localhost`) so Docker containers can reach it. Models: `nomic-embed-text` (274MB), `bge-m3`, `qwen2.5-coder:7b`. Ollama is also a service with restart policy.

**Cross-session recall:** "María asked about tent X last week" → "María is now asking about camping stoves — connect the interest."

**LightRAG knowledge base** is seeded on startup via `/seed` endpoint with client's products, FAQs, and policies. Tested query for "devolucion" correctly returns 30-day return policy.

**RAM overhead:** LightRAG + Ollama consumes ~2-4GB total. Ensure the VPS has headroom before starting.

## Stack

| Component | What |
|-----------|------|
|-----------|------|
| **Evolution API** v2.3.7+ | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — one instance per client phone |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web control at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager |
| **FastAPI** webhook receiver | Receives Evolution `onMessage` events, routes per client |
| **pywa** v3.9.0 | Python WhatsApp Cloud API library (installed, for Meta Cloud API) |
| **Traefik** | Exposes webhook receiver at `whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud` |
| **AI** | DeepSeek (primary, with function calling), Groq via LiteLLM (fallback) |
| **PostgreSQL** | Shared — Evolution message store + HermesClient/HermesContact tables |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware memory, per-client workspaces |

## Deployment (Docker Swarm)

```bash
cd /root/whatsapp-ai-agent

# Build with version tag
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.1 .

# Deploy with FULL env vars inlined (env_file does NOT work with Swarm deploy)
# The docker-compose.yml must have `environment:` block, NOT `env_file:`
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml whatsapp-ai-stack

# IMPORTANT: env_file is IGNORED by Swarm. All env vars must be inlined
# in the docker-compose.yml `environment:` section.

# To force an update after rebuilding, ALWAYS bump the image tag:
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 .
docker service update --image whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai

# IMPORTANT: `docker service update --force` does NOT restart containers
# if the image tag hasn't changed. Always use a new tag.

# If container fails with "password authentication failed for user postgres"
# even after passing PG_DSN in environment, the env vars aren't reaching the container.
# Use `docker service logs whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai --tail 20` to check.
```

### Required Environment Variables (must be inlined in compose)

```yaml
environment:
  EVOLUTION_API_URL: https://evolution.sunstein.cloud
  EVOLUTION_API_KEY: "<full key from container env>"
  DEEPSEEK_API_KEY: "<key>"
  DEEPSEEK_MODEL: deepseek-chat
  PG_DSN: "[REDACTED_DB_URL]

8000"
  MAX_HISTORY: "50"
```

The `.env` file is useful for `docker run` but NOT for `docker stack deploy`.

### pip Install on This Server

```bash
# The system Python on this VPS requires --break-system-packages
pip install --break-system-packages <package>
```

### Traefik Config (after switching to Swarm mode)

The old `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` pointing to `127.0.0.1:8000` must be deleted when moving to Docker Swarm. Swarm-mode Traefik auto-discovers services via `traefik.*` labels on the service. File-path configs override Swarm discovery. Remove:

```bash
rm -f /opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml
```

## Client Onboarding

One API call creates the Evolution instance, sets the webhook, and returns a QR code.

```bash
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Mi Tienda",
    "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
    "mode": "ventas"
  }'
```

Response includes a QR code. Client scans with WhatsApp Business app → instance goes `open` → webhook active.

### Modes (built-in prompts)

| Mode | Use |
|------|-----|
| `default` | General customer service |
| `ventas` | Sales assistant — products, pricing, buying process |
| `soporte` | Technical support — returns, warranties, tracking |
| Custom `system_prompt` | Override with any text |

## API Reference

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Service status |
| `/clients` | GET | List all clients from Postgres |
| `/clients` | POST | Onboard new client (creates Evolution instance + webhook + Postgres row + optional LightRAG workspace) |
| `/clients/cloud` | POST | Create Cloud API client (requires CLOUD_API_TOKEN + CLOUD_PHONE_ID env vars). Returns QR or pairing code instructions. |
| `/clients/{instance}` | GET | Client details from Postgres |
| `/clients/{instance}` | DELETE | Remove client from DB + Evolution |
| `/clients/{instance}/personality` | PATCH | Update mode/system_prompt/repo_path |
| `/clients/{instance}/qr` | GET | Regenerate QR code for re-pairing |
| `/webhook/{instance}` | POST | Evolution fires this on each incoming message |
| `/clients/{instance}/chats` | GET | List all contacts with message counts & date ranges |
| `/clients/{instance}/messages/{jid}` | GET | Get past messages for a specific contact (bare number or full JID) |
| `/clients/{instance}/import` | POST | Batch-import past chat history into LightRAG memory |

### POST /clients payload

```json
{
  "name": "string (required) — client display name",
  "phone": "string (required) — raw digits, no + or spaces",
  "mode": "string (optional) — default|ventas|soporte",
  "system_prompt": "string (optional) — overrides mode",
  "repo_path": "string (optional) — path to client's repo for knowledge base",
  "lightrag_workspace": "string (optional) — workspace name for tenant isolation"
}
```

Response includes `lightrag_workspace` — used for tenant-isolated memory in LightRAG. If not specified, auto-generated from phone number.

### PATCH /clients/{instance}/personality

Update a client's AI personality without recreating the instance:

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/client-store-1234/personality \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"mode": "ventas", "repo_path": "elviajero-comercio"}'
```

Valid fields: `mode`, `system_prompt`, `repo_path`.

### POST /clients/cloud

For WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) instead of Evolution Baileys. Requires Meta Business setup:

```json
{
  "name": "Mi Tienda",
  "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
  "mode": "ventas"
}
```

If CLOUD_API_TOKEN is configured, attempts Evolution instance creation first. Falls back to pairing code instructions. Cloud API clients get their own LightRAG workspace.

## AI Backend

The service tries providers in order:

1. **DeepSeek** (`deepseek-chat` via `api.deepseek.com/v1`) — $0.14/M input tokens (primary, low latency). **Supports function calling** with `tools` parameter.
2. **Groq** (`groq-llama` via local LiteLLM proxy on :4000) — free tier, 6 models available, no function calling.
3. Returns error message if all fail.

### Tool Calling (v3.0+)

The agent uses DeepSeek's native function calling with a two-pass execution:

```python
# First call: LLM decides which tool(s) to call
tools = [
    {
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": "lookup_product",
            "description": "Search products by name or category",
            "parameters": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {"query": {"type": "string"}},
                "required": ["query"]
            }
        }
    },
    # ... get_business_hours, get_shipping_policy, get_return_policy
]

# Second call: LLM synthesizes response with tool results
messages.append(tool_result_message)
resp2 = await client.post(..., json={"messages": messages})
```

Supported tools: `lookup_product`, `get_business_hours`, `get_shipping_policy`, `get_return_policy`. Tools are defined in code, not fetched from a registry.

### Direct Message Sending via Instance Token

Evolution API v2.3.7 has two auth mechanisms. The global API key may be restricted — many endpoints return 401. For sending messages, use the **instance-specific token** stored in Postgres:

```bash
docker exec postgres_postgres.* psql -U postgres -d evolution \
  -c "SELECT token FROM \"Instance\" WHERE name='hermes-whatsapp';"

curl -X POST "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/message/sendText/hermes-whatsapp" \
  -H "apikey: $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"number": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "text": "Hello", "delay": 1000}'
```

Response includes `"status": "PENDING"` when instance is reconnecting. Message delivers once reconnected.

**Fallback chain verification:** All 3 providers tested and confirmed working in this session. LiteLLM/Groq was reconfigured from OpenRouter to reduce costs. Cron jobs migrated from OpenRouter/Anthropic to DeepSeek.

Before each AI call, the service queries LightRAG for entity-aware context about the customer and conversation topic. This context is prepended to the system prompt. The LightRAG knowledge base is pre-seeded with client products, FAQs, and policies.

Conversation history is stored per client (in-memory dict, last 50 messages). Postgres persistence is planned.

### Session Context Chain (Order of Operations)

1. Receive webhook message from Evolution
2. Filter `fromMe: true` messages (echo prevention) and group messages unless configured otherwise
3. Check in-memory conversation history for this remoteJid (last 50 messages)
4. **NEW: Query Evolution Postgres DB for this contact's past messages (last 15 messages, not in memory)**
5. Query LightRAG for relevant entity context about customer + topic
6. Build prompt: system_prompt + RAG context + Evolution history + conversation memory + new message
7. Call AI (try DeepSeek → Groq → Ollama fallback chain)
8. Send response via Evolution API sendText
9. Store message + response in conversation history
10. Optionally update LightRAG with new entities from the exchange

### Batch Import (Post-Deployment)

After deploying a new instance, import its past messages into LightRAG:

```bash
# Import top 50 individual chats (500 messages each)
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/hermes-whatsapp/import \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"max_per_chat": 500, "max_chats": 50}'
```

This feeds ~25,000 messages as entity-aware LightRAG chunks. Import is idempotent — re-running creates additional chunks but doesn't corrupt existing memory.

## Evolution Manager UI

Accessible at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager`. Login with the **full** API key (retrieved from the container env, not the truncated .env version). From the UI you can:
- View/QR-reconnect instances
- Browse chats and contacts
- Configure webhooks and integrations

See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`.

## Postgres Column Quoting (CRITICAL)

Evolution's Prisma schema uses camelCase column names. Postgres lowercases unquoted identifiers. **Every** camelCase reference in SQL must be double-quoted:

```sql
-- WRONG: pushName becomes pushname → error
SELECT pushName FROM "Message" WHERE pushName IS NOT NULL;

-- RIGHT: 
SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message" WHERE "pushName" IS NOT NULL;
```

**Bug we found:** Both `_enrich_chat_name()` and `_fetch_chat_messages()` had unquoted `pushName` in their SQL. Symptoms: `column "pushname" does not exist`. Fix all locations with a grep:

```bash
grep -n 'SELECT.*pushName\|WHERE.*pushName' app/main.py
# Should only show "pushName" references
```

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for complete quoting reference.

## Pitfalls

1. **`connectionStatus: connecting` is NORMAL for unpaired instances** — instances with `connecting` status are waiting for QR scan, not broken. Even a 401-disconnected instance shows `connecting`. Do NOT assume failure unless `disconnectionReasonCode` or `close` status is present. The `fetchInstances` response with `connectionStatus: connecting` + `ownerJid` populated + `disconnectionReasonCode: 401` = logged out, needs re-pairing. No `ownerJid` + `connecting` = never paired, needs first QR scan.

2. **Evolution instance creation requires `integration: WHATSAPP-BAILEYS`** — without it the API returns `400 Invalid integration`.

3. **Webhook URL must be publicly reachable** — Evolution runs in Docker overlay, can't reach `localhost`. Must use Traefik public URL (e.g. `[REDACTED_WEBHOOK] **QR codes expire in ~45 seconds** — be ready to scan immediately. Regenerate with `GET /clients/{instance}/qr`.

5. **WhatsApp max 4 linked devices** per phone number. Client may need to remove old links.

6. **Manager UI needs full-length API key** — The `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` shown in `docker service inspect` is truncated. Get it via `docker exec <container> sh -c 'echo $AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY'`. See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`.

7. **Swarm ignores `env_file`** — Docker Swarm deploy does NOT read `.env` files. All environment variables must be passed inline under `environment:` in the compose file.

8. **Postgres camelCase columns must be double-quoted** — `SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message"`, not `pushName` or `pushname`. See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md`.

9. **Instance shows `open` but messages stay PENDING** — stale session. Fix: logout (`DELETE /instance/logout/{name}`) → fresh QR (`GET /instance/connect/{name}`) → re-scan.

10. **Sending messages requires instance token, not global key** — Global `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` returns 401 for `/message/sendText`. Use per-instance token from `SELECT token FROM "Instance"`. Response `PENDING` is normal — delivers on reconnect.

11. **Image tag must change on each deploy** — Swarm doesn't detect local image changes for same tag. Bump tag and use `docker service update --image <name>:<new-tag> <service>`.

12. **Traefik file config blocks Swarm discovery** — Delete `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` if it exists when using Swarm-mode Traefik labels. File providers override Swarm auto-discovery. See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.

13. **Evolution API v2.3.7 has NO pairing code endpoint** — `POST /instance/pairingCode/*`, `POST /instance/requestPairingCode/*` all return 404. QR scanning is the ONLY connection method until v2.4+.

14. **Import: "column pushname does not exist"** — camelCase quoting bug. Grep all SQL: `grep -n 'SELECT.*pushName\|WHERE.*pushName' app/main.py`. Every camelCase reference needs double quotes.

## Research-Based Upgrade Roadmap

See `references/evolution-upgrade-research-2026-05.md` for full findings. High-priority next builds:

1. **LightRAG** — ✓ DEPLOYED: entity-aware memory across sessions (Docker on :9623, query before each AI call)
2. **Custom Business MCP** — product/FAQ/orders lookup tools for the AI to call during conversations
3. **Session persistence** — in-memory dict → Postgres (already running)
4. **SOUL.md personalities** — proven formula for sales/support/default personas
5. **Supabase MCP** — real-time product/order queries for AI
6. **Langfuse observability** — conversation tracing, cost analytics (pending)

{"success": true, "name": "evolution-whatsapp-ai-agent", "file": "references/evolution-upgrade-research-2026-05.md", "content": "# Evolution WhatsApp AI Agent — Upgrade Research (May 2026)

Findings from Hermes ecosystem research for improving the multi-client WhatsApp AI bot.

## 1. LightRAG — Entity-Aware Long-Term Memory

Graph RAG that knows entity relationships. Remembers "María asked about tent X last week" and connects to "María asking about camping stoves today." Runs as Docker on port 9623, queried via HTTP before each AI call. Documented in optimization-guide Part 3.

## 2. Supabase MCP — Real-Time Product & Order Data

Exposes Supabase tables (products, orders, customers) as AI-callable tools. URL already in config (mcp.supabase.com) but needs auth. Eliminates hallucinated product/pricing responses.

## 3. Custom Business MCP — Client-Specific Knowledge Base

FastAPI server exposing: `lookup_product`, `get_shipping_policy`, `check_business_hours`, `return_policy`. Unique per client instance.

## 4. Session Persistence (In-Memory → Postgres)

Postgres already running. Store conversation history for analytics + cross-session recall.

## 5. SOUL.md Personality System

From optimization guide Part 2. Create 3 profiles: Ventas (sales, upselling, objection handling), Soporte (troubleshooting, escalation), Default (friendly, general info).

## 6. Langfuse Observability

Traces conversation quality, token cost, conversion analytics.

## Priority

1. LightRAG + memory (2h, high impact) 
2. Custom Business MCP (2h, high impact)
3. Session persistence (1h, medium)
4. SOUL.md personalities (30m, medium)
5. Supabase MCP (30m, medium)
6. Langfuse (1h, nice)
", "file_type": ".md"}

npm run build && docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod . && docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml nexa

Most international (4 locales). Relocation focus. Document-heavy. WhatsApp contact.

It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,950 chars total]

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter (all). Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) - shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`

- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content still lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) now comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- SQLite database for orders and admin
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

{"content": "WhatsApp Connect
================
Subdomain: whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com
Purpose: Admin login + connect WhatsApp Business via Evolution API

Architecture:
- Single-page app served by a simple HTTP server on port 8990
- Supabase Auth for admin login (any admin across all client projects)
- Evolution API for instance management (create, QR, check status)
- Traefik route via /opt/traefik/dynamic/wa-connect.yml

Files:
- public/index.html — Full SPA, no build step
- server.js — Node.js static file server + API proxy (avoids CORS)
- Dockerfile — containerize
- docker-compose.yml — Swarm deploy

Evolution instances naming convention:
  {client-slug}-{4-char-hex}
Examples:
  elviajero-a3f2
  golden-visa-b7c1
  nexa-d9e4

Admin login:
- Uses same Supabase project as all client sites (qyvokpribmbrosafntqa)
- Admin check: profiles table WHERE email = user.email AND role = 'admin'
- redirect to /admin/dashboard on success

Dashboard shows:
- List of all Evolution instances
- For each: status (open/connecting/close), QR (if not connected), linked phone
- Button: "Connect WhatsApp" → generates QR
- Button: "Disconnect" → logs out instance
- Link to: manage system prompts, view webhook status

Tech decisions:
- Pure HTML/CSS/JS (no React, no build step)
- Supabase JS client loaded from CDN
- Tailwind CSS loaded from CDN (for styling)
- Evolution API calls proxied through server.js to avoid CORS issues
", "path": "/root/whatsapp-connect/ARCHITECTURE.md"}

{"content": "services:
  connect:
    image: whatsapp-connect:v1
    ports:
      - target: 8990
    environment:
      - EVOLUTION_API_KEY=[REDACTED_TOKEN]
      - API_BASE=https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
      labels:
        traefik.enable: "true"
        traefik.swarm.network: "agent-net"
        traefik.http.routers.wa-connect.rule: "Host(`whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com`)"
        traefik.http.routers.wa-connect.entrypoints: "websecure"
        traefik.http.routers.wa-connect.tls.certresolver: "letsencrypt"
        traefik.http.services.wa-connect.loadbalancer.server.port: "8990"
    networks:
      - agent-net

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
", "path": "/root/whatsapp-connect/docker-compose.yml"}

Done. Here's the summary:

**Deployed:** `whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com`

**What it does:**
- Login with any admin email/password (from the shared Supabase project)
- Shows all connected Evolution instances with live status
- "Connect WhatsApp" button generates a QR that auto-refreshes every 10s
- "Disconnect" to log out a session

**To make it live:**
1. Add `whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com` A record → `72.61.44.159` in Cloudflare DNS
2. SSL cert auto-provisions via Let's Encrypt within ~60s
3. Open the URL and log in

**Temp workaround** (no SSL): http://72.61.44.159:8990/

The app uses Tailwind CSS and Supabase JS from CDN — no build step. The server proxies API requests to avoid CORS issues.

Those are Cloudflare's IPv6 addresses — proxied mode. That means Cloudflare is proxying. Since it's proxied, the SSL handshake goes to Cloudflare, and Cloudflare attempts to reach your origin. But Traefik's Let's Encrypt can't challenge because Cloudflare already swallowed port 80.

Change the Cloudflare DNS to **DNS only** (gray cloud) in your Cloudflare dashboard, then Let's Encrypt will provision the cert. After it's done, you can switch back to proxied (orange cloud).

Or alternatively, I can change the Traefik config to use the `tls.challenge` with Cloudflare's API, but that's more complex. Simpler: **gray cloud for 30 seconds**, then orange cloud back.

No Traefik log entries at all for this domain — it's being fully handled by Cloudflare. Cloudflare proxied mode means Cloudflare sits in front and tries to reach origin at 72.61.44.159:443 but there's no SSL there since Traefik couldn't get a cert (because Cloudflare was blocking the ACME challenge).

**Fix**: Change the cloud to gray (DNS only) in Cloudflare, wait 30s, then it'll work. Or I can set up a self-signed cert so Cloudflare proxied can reach the origin via HTTPS.

Traefik is responding at 72.61.44.159:443 but with a 404 (no matching Host rule since Cloudflare is forwarding the request with the origin host header).

The issue is clear: Cloudflare proxied mode needs the origin to have a valid cert. But Let's Encrypt couldn't issue one because Cloudflare was in the way.

Change the Cloudflare DNS record to **DNS only** (gray cloud). After 30 seconds, it'll work at https://whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com/ and Let's Encrypt will auto-provision the cert.

If you want to keep it proxied (orange cloud), I can generate a self-signed cert on the server, but that's hacky. Gray cloud for 30s then back to orange is the clean approach.
done

Actually, the real issue is simpler — the app uses the truncated anon key. The correct full anon key is: `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.[REDACTED_TOKEN][REDACTED_TOKEN]0.[REDACTED_TOKEN]`

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:

  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.

# Evolution WhatsApp AI Agent

Deploy a multi-tenant WhatsApp AI support and sales agent. Each client links their WhatsApp Business number → Evolution API instance → webhook to a FastAPI service → AI response back via Evolution.

## Architecture (v3.0.1 — May 2026)

```
Client WhatsApp Business → Evolution API Instance
    → POST /webhook/{instance} (incoming message)
        → FastAPI router → client system prompt + conversation history
            → LightRAG query (entity-aware memory, port 9623)  ← also has PAST CHAT HISTORY context
            → Evolution DB query (past messages for this contact)  ← NEW in v2.1.0
                → AI (DeepSeek with tool-calling → Groq fallback)
                    → Function execution (products, hours, policies)
                    → Second-pass AI with tool results
                        → Evolution API /message/sendText → Customer

                    ← Meanwhile, LightRAG gets batch-imported with past chats
                       via POST /clients/{instance}/import

                    ← Postgres persists clients in "HermesClient" table
                       per-client personality via PATCH /clients/{inst}/personality
```

### Hermes Integration

The WhatsApp agent runs alongside Hermes Agent but NOT as a gateway adapter. In Hermes v0.11.0, `whatsapp_provider: evolution` is a no-op — the gateway only supports the built-in Baileys bridge. Evolution runs as a standalone service:

 | Component | Role | Port |
|-----------|------|------|
| **Evolution API** | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — message send/receive | 8080 |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web interface at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager | (browser) |
| **FastAPI (this service)** | Webhook receiver + client management + history import | 8000 |
| **Evolution Postgres** | Message store (43k+ messages, 409 contacts) — accessed directly | 5432 |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware long-term memory per contact | 9623 |
| **Hermes-lcm plugin** | Lossless DAG context for long conversations | (plugin, no port) |
| **Skill Factory** | Auto-generates improved sales/support scripts | (plugin, reload on /reset) |
| **Traefik** | Public ingress at whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud | 443 |

### History Import (New in v2.1.0)

Evolution API v2.3.7 has **no REST endpoint** to retrieve past messages. All `/chat/findMessages`, `/chat/getMessages`, `/message/list` endpoints return 404. The only way to access stored messages is **direct Postgres connection** to Evolution's database.

The agent now does this:
1. Connects to `postgres:5432/evolution` via asyncpg
2. Queries the `Message` table (43,741 messages, back to June 2024) using `"remoteJid"` from the `key` JSONB column
3. Before each AI response, fetches recent past messages for that contact from Evolution's DB
4. Can batch-import entire chat histories into LightRAG via `POST /clients/{instance}/import`

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for full table structure and query patterns.

### LightRAG Integration (Deployed)

LightRAG runs as a Docker container on port 9623 with its own restart policy and volume persistence. Before each AI response, it's queried for entity-aware context:

```python
rag_url = "http://lightrag:9623"  # Docker network; also reachable as localhost outside container
rag_payload = {
    "query": "what does the customer know about? what products have they asked about?",
    "mode": "hybrid"  # local + global graph search
}
rag_response = requests.post(f"{rag_url}/query", json=rag_payload)
```

**What changed this round (May 5):** Migrated from raw systemd process to Docker container with restart-always policy. LightRAG now survives host reboots. Uses DeepSeek (`deepseek-chat`) as LLM backend (via `LLM_BINDING_HOST` env) and `bge-m3` for 1024-dimensional embeddings via Ollama.

**Ollama runs on host** bound to `0.0.0.0:11434` (not `localhost`) so Docker containers can reach it. Models: `nomic-embed-text` (274MB), `bge-m3`, `qwen2.5-coder:7b`. Ollama is also a service with restart policy.

**Cross-session recall:** "María asked about tent X last week" → "María is now asking about camping stoves — connect the interest."

**LightRAG knowledge base** is seeded on startup via `/seed` endpoint with client's products, FAQs, and policies. Tested query for "devolucion" correctly returns 30-day return policy.

**RAM overhead:** LightRAG + Ollama consumes ~2-4GB total. Ensure the VPS has headroom before starting.

## Stack

| Component | What |
|-----------|------|
|-----------|------|
| **Evolution API** v2.3.7+ | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — one instance per client phone |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web control at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager |
| **FastAPI** webhook receiver | Receives Evolution `onMessage` events, routes per client |
| **pywa** v3.9.0 | Python WhatsApp Cloud API library (installed, for Meta Cloud API) |
| **Traefik** | Exposes webhook receiver at `whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud` |
| **AI** | DeepSeek (primary, with function calling), Groq via LiteLLM (fallback) |
| **PostgreSQL** | Shared — Evolution message store + HermesClient/HermesContact tables |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware memory, per-client workspaces |

## Deployment (Docker Swarm)

```bash
cd /root/whatsapp-ai-agent

# Build with version tag
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.1 .

# Deploy with FULL env vars inlined (env_file does NOT work with Swarm deploy)
# The docker-compose.yml must have `environment:` block, NOT `env_file:`
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml whatsapp-ai-stack

# IMPORTANT: env_file is IGNORED by Swarm. All env vars must be inlined
# in the docker-compose.yml `environment:` section.

# To force an update after rebuilding, ALWAYS bump the image tag:
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 .
docker service update --image whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai

# IMPORTANT: `docker service update --force` does NOT restart containers
# if the image tag hasn't changed. Always use a new tag.

# If container fails with "password authentication failed for user postgres"
# even after passing PG_DSN in environment, the env vars aren't reaching the container.
# Use `docker service logs whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai --tail 20` to check.
```

### Required Environment Variables (must be inlined in compose)

```yaml
environment:
  EVOLUTION_API_URL: https://evolution.sunstein.cloud
  EVOLUTION_API_KEY: "<full key from container env>"
  DEEPSEEK_API_KEY: "<key>"
  DEEPSEEK_MODEL: deepseek-chat
  PG_DSN: "[REDACTED_DB_URL]

8000" MAX_HISTORY: "50" ``` The `.env` file is useful for `docker run` but NOT for `docker stack deploy`. ### pip Install on This Server ```bash # The system Python on this VPS requires --break-system-packages pip install --break-system-packages ``` ### Traefik Config (after switching to Swarm mode) The old `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` pointing to `127.0.0.1:8000` must be deleted when moving to Docker Swarm. Swarm-mode Traefik auto-discovers services via `traefik.*` labels on the service. File-path configs override Swarm discovery. Remove: ```bash rm -f /opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml ``` ## Client Onboarding One API call creates the Evolution instance, sets the webhook, and returns a QR code. ```bash curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "name": "Mi Tienda", "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "mode": "ventas" }' ``` Response includes a QR code. Client scans with WhatsApp Business app → instance goes `open` → webhook active. ### Modes (built-in prompts) | Mode | Use | |------|-----| | `default` | General customer service | | `ventas` | Sales assistant — products, pricing, buying process | | `soporte` | Technical support — returns, warranties, tracking | | Custom `system_prompt` | Override with any text | ## API Reference | Endpoint | Method | Description | |----------|--------|-------------| | `/health` | GET | Service status | | `/clients` | GET | List all clients from Postgres | | `/clients` | POST | Onboard new client (creates Evolution instance + webhook + Postgres row + optional LightRAG workspace) | | `/clients/cloud` | POST | Create Cloud API client (requires CLOUD_API_TOKEN + CLOUD_PHONE_ID env vars). Returns QR or pairing code instructions. | | `/clients/{instance}` | GET | Client details from Postgres | | `/clients/{instance}` | DELETE | Remove client from DB + Evolution | | `/clients/{instance}/personality` | PATCH | Update mode/system_prompt/repo_path | | `/clients/{instance}/qr` | GET | Regenerate QR code for re-pairing | | `/webhook/{instance}` | POST | Evolution fires this on each incoming message | | `/clients/{instance}/chats` | GET | List all contacts with message counts & date ranges | | `/clients/{instance}/messages/{jid}` | GET | Get past messages for a specific contact (bare number or full JID) | | `/clients/{instance}/import` | POST | Batch-import past chat history into LightRAG memory | ### POST /clients payload ```json { "name": "string (required) — client display name", "phone": "string (required) — raw digits, no + or spaces", "mode": "string (optional) — default|ventas|soporte", "system_prompt": "string (optional) — overrides mode", "repo_path": "string (optional) — path to client's repo for knowledge base", "lightrag_workspace": "string (optional) — workspace name for tenant isolation" } ``` Response includes `lightrag_workspace` — used for tenant-isolated memory in LightRAG. If not specified, auto-generated from phone number. ### PATCH /clients/{instance}/personality Update a client's AI personality without recreating the instance: ```bash curl -X PATCH https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/client-store-1234/personality \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"mode": "ventas", "repo_path": "elviajero-comercio"}' ``` Valid fields: `mode`, `system_prompt`, `repo_path`. ### POST /clients/cloud For WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) instead of Evolution Baileys. Requires Meta Business setup: ```json { "name": "Mi Tienda", "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "mode": "ventas" } ``` If CLOUD_API_TOKEN is configured, attempts Evolution instance creation first. Falls back to pairing code instructions. Cloud API clients get their own LightRAG workspace. ## AI Backend The service tries providers in order: 1. **DeepSeek** (`deepseek-chat` via `api.deepseek.com/v1`) — $0.14/M input tokens (primary, low latency). **Supports function calling** with `tools` parameter. 2. **Groq** (`groq-llama` via local LiteLLM proxy on :4000) — free tier, 6 models available, no function calling. 3. Returns error message if all fail. ### Tool Calling (v3.0+) The agent uses DeepSeek's native function calling with a two-pass execution: ```python # First call: LLM decides which tool(s) to call tools = [ { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "lookup_product", "description": "Search products by name or category", "parameters": { "type": "object", "properties": {"query": {"type": "string"}}, "required": ["query"] } } }, # ... get_business_hours, get_shipping_policy, get_return_policy ] # Second call: LLM synthesizes response with tool results messages.append(tool_result_message) resp2 = await client.post(..., json={"messages": messages}) ``` Supported tools: `lookup_product`, `get_business_hours`, `get_shipping_policy`, `get_return_policy`. Tools are defined in code, not fetched from a registry. ### Direct Message Sending via Instance Token Evolution API v2.3.7 has two auth mechanisms. The global API key may be restricted — many endpoints return 401. For sending messages, use the **instance-specific token** stored in Postgres: ```bash docker exec postgres_postgres.* psql -U postgres -d evolution \ -c "SELECT token FROM \"Instance\" WHERE name='hermes-whatsapp';" curl -X POST "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/message/sendText/hermes-whatsapp" \ -H "apikey: $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"number": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "text": "Hello", "delay": 1000}' ``` Response includes `"status": "PENDING"` when instance is reconnecting. Message delivers once reconnected. **Fallback chain verification:** All 3 providers tested and confirmed working in this session. LiteLLM/Groq was reconfigured from OpenRouter to reduce costs. Cron jobs migrated from OpenRouter/Anthropic to DeepSeek. Before each AI call, the service queries LightRAG for entity-aware context about the customer and conversation topic. This context is prepended to the system prompt. The LightRAG knowledge base is pre-seeded with client products, FAQs, and policies. Conversation history is stored per client (in-memory dict, last 50 messages). Postgres persistence is planned. ### Session Context Chain (Order of Operations) 1. Receive webhook message from Evolution 2. Filter `fromMe: true` messages (echo prevention) and group messages unless configured otherwise 3. Check in-memory conversation history for this remoteJid (last 50 messages) 4. **NEW: Query Evolution Postgres DB for this contact's past messages (last 15 messages, not in memory)** 5. Query LightRAG for relevant entity context about customer + topic 6. Build prompt: system_prompt + RAG context + Evolution history + conversation memory + new message 7. Call AI (try DeepSeek → Groq → Ollama fallback chain) 8. Send response via Evolution API sendText 9. Store message + response in conversation history 10. Optionally update LightRAG with new entities from the exchange ### Batch Import (Post-Deployment) After deploying a new instance, import its past messages into LightRAG: ```bash # Import top 50 individual chats (500 messages each) curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/hermes-whatsapp/import \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"max_per_chat": 500, "max_chats": 50}' ``` This feeds ~25,000 messages as entity-aware LightRAG chunks. Import is idempotent — re-running creates additional chunks but doesn't corrupt existing memory. ## Evolution Manager UI Accessible at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager`. Login with the **full** API key (retrieved from the container env, not the truncated .env version). From the UI you can: - View/QR-reconnect instances - Browse chats and contacts - Configure webhooks and integrations See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`. ## Postgres Column Quoting (CRITICAL) Evolution's Prisma schema uses camelCase column names. Postgres lowercases unquoted identifiers. **Every** camelCase reference in SQL must be double-quoted: ```sql -- WRONG: pushName becomes pushname → error SELECT pushName FROM "Message" WHERE pushName IS NOT NULL; -- RIGHT: SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message" WHERE "pushName" IS NOT NULL; ``` **Bug we found:** Both `_enrich_chat_name()` and `_fetch_chat_messages()` had unquoted `pushName` in their SQL. Symptoms: `column "pushname" does not exist`. Fix all locations with a grep: ```bash grep -n 'SELECT.*pushName\|WHERE.*pushName' app/main.py # Should only show "pushName" references ``` See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for complete quoting reference. ## Pitfalls 1. **`connectionStatus: connecting` is NORMAL for unpaired instances** — instances with `connecting` status are waiting for QR scan, not broken. Even a 401-disconnected instance shows `connecting`. Do NOT assume failure unless `disconnectionReasonCode` or `close` status is present. The `fetchInstances` response with `connectionStatus: connecting` + `ownerJid` populated + `disconnectionReasonCode: 401` = logged out, needs re-pairing. No `ownerJid` + `connecting` = never paired, needs first QR scan. ## Pitfalls 1. **`connectionStatus: connecting` is NORMAL for unpaired instances** — instances with `connecting` status are waiting for QR scan, not broken. Even a 401-disconnected instance shows `connecting`. Do NOT assume failure unless `disconnectionReasonCode` or `close` status is present. The `fetchInstances` response with `connectionStatus: connecting` + `ownerJid` populated + `disconnectionReasonCode: 401` = logged out, needs re-pairing. No `ownerJid` + `connecting` = never paired, needs first QR scan. 2. **Evolution instance creation requires `integration: WHATSAPP-BAILEYS`** — without it the API returns `400 Invalid integration`. 3. **Webhook URL must be publicly reachable** — Evolution runs in Docker overlay, can't reach `localhost`. Must use Traefik public URL (e.g. `[REDACTED_WEBHOOK] **QR codes expire in ~45-60 seconds** — `GET /instance/connect/{name}` returns a `count` field (seconds remaining, starts ~65-72). You have ~5-10s to process and serve. By the time you write to disk + serve via CDN, it's usually expired. See **QR Delivery** section below for workarounds. 5. **WhatsApp max 4 linked devices** per phone number. Client may need to remove old links. 6. **Manager UI needs full-length API key** — The `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` shown in `docker service inspect` is truncated. Get it via `docker exec sh -c 'echo $AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY'`. See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`. 7. **Swarm ignores `env_file`** — Docker Swarm deploy does NOT read `.env` files. All environment variables must be passed inline under `environment:` in the compose file. 8. **Postgres camelCase columns must be double-quoted** — `SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message"`, not `pushName` or `pushname`. See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md`. 9. **Instance shows `open` but messages stay PENDING** — stale session. Fix: logout (`DELETE /instance/logout/{name}`) → fresh QR (`GET /instance/connect/{name}`) → re-scan. 10. **Sending messages requires instance token, not global key** — Global `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` returns 401 for `/message/sendText`. Use per-instance token from `SELECT token FROM "Instance"`. Response `PENDING` is normal — delivers on reconnect. 11. **Image tag must change on each deploy** — Swarm doesn't detect local image changes for same tag. Bump tag and use `docker service update --image : `. 12. **Traefik file config blocks Swarm discovery** — Delete `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` if it exists when using Swarm-mode Traefik labels. File providers override Swarm auto-discovery. See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`. 13. **Evolution API v2.3.7 has NO pairing code endpoint** — `POST /instance/pairingCode/*`, `POST /instance/requestPairingCode/*` all return 404. QR scanning is the ONLY connection method until v2.4+. 14. **Import: "column pushname does not exist"** — camelCase quoting bug. Grep all SQL: `grep -n 'SELECT.*pushName\\|WHERE.*pushName' app/main.py`. Every camelCase reference needs double quotes. ## QR Delivery (Critical timing problem) The QR from `GET /instance/connect/{name}` expires in ~5-10s by the time you download, decode, and serve it. Three delivery strategies ranked by speed: **Strategy A — Running HTTP server (fastest):** ```bash # Start a python HTTP server cd /tmp && python3 -m http.server 8099 & # In a single command, generate QR and write the HTML TOKEN="" curl -s "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connect/hermes-whatsapp" \ -H "apikey: $TOKEN" | python3 -c " import sys, json data = json.load(sys.stdin) with open('/tmp/qr-page.html', 'w') as f: f.write(f'

Scan with WhatsApp Business

') print(f'QR count: {data.get(\"count\",0)}') " # User opens http://:8099/qr-page.html on phone ``` **Strategy B — Auto-refreshing page (best for user):** Same as A but the HTML includes `` so the page refreshes every 12s with a fresh QR. User just keeps the page open. **Strategy C — Shopify-blocked domains trap:** If the target domain is behind Shopify CDN (like `nexaparaguay.com`), all requests including QR images get intercepted and show "This store is currently unavailable." **Do NOT use Shopify-proxied domains** for QR serving. Use a non-Shopify domain or raw IP:port. **Known non-Shopify domains in this stack:** - `nexa.paragu-ai.com` — routes to nexa_web Docker service (Next.js), but static `/public` files aren't served until the container is rebuilt with them - `72.61.44.159:` — raw server IP, works for quick http.server - `sunstein.cloud` — for production services ## Research-Based Upgrade Roadmap See `references/evolution-upgrade-research-2026-05.md` for full findings. {"success": true, "name": "evolution-whatsapp-ai-agent", "file": "references/whatsapp-connect-spa.md", "content": "# WhatsApp Connect SPA — Admin QR Portal Deployed at `/root/whatsapp-connect/` on the VPS. Live at `https://whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com` (requires DNS: A record → 72.61.44.159). ## What It Is A standalone Node.js static server + API proxy SPA that lets any admin user (via Supabase Auth) log in and connect WhatsApp Business numbers through Evolution API. No build step — pure HTML/JS. ## Architecture ``` User browser → whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com → Traefik (Swarm, TLS via letsencryptresolver) → wa-connect_connect:8990 (Node.js server) → serves public/index.html (login + dashboard) → proxies /api/* to internal services → /api/clients → whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients → /api/token/:instance → Evolution instance token resolution → /api/instance/:name/status → Evolution connectionState → Supabase JS CDN (browser-side auth) ``` ## Stack | Component | Detail | |-----------|--------| | **Server** | Node.js 20 (http module, no express) | | **Proxy** | Inline in server.js — fetches to Evolution API + WhatsApp AI API | | **Frontend** | Vanilla HTML + Tailwind CSS CDN + Supabase JS CDN | | **Auth** | Supabase Auth (project qyvokpribmbrosafntqa) — checks `profiles.role = admin` | | **Port** | 8990 (internal, exposed via Swarm labels) | | **Deployment** | Docker Swarm service `wa-connect_connect` | ## Files ``` /root/whatsapp-connect/ ├── public/index.html — SPA: login form + dashboard with client cards + QR modal ├── server.js — HTTP server + API proxy ├── Dockerfile — node:20-alpine ├── docker-compose.yml — Swarm deploy + Traefik labels └── ARCHITECTURE.md — Design notes ``` ## Key Patterns ### Traefik Swarm Labels Must use `traefik.swarm.*` not `traefik.docker.*` (Traefik on this server uses Swarm provider): ```yaml deploy: labels: traefik.enable: "true" traefik.swarm.network: "agent-net" traefik.http.routers.wa-connect.rule: "Host(`whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com`)" traefik.http.routers.wa-connect.entrypoints: "websecure" traefik.http.routers.wa-connect.tls.certresolver: "letsencryptresolver" traefik.http.services.wa-connect.loadbalancer.server.port: "8990" ``` ### Cert Resolver Name The Traefik instance uses `letsencryptresolver` (not `letsencrypt`). Using the wrong name causes `Router uses a nonexistent certificate resolver` error. ### API Proxy for CORS The SPA calls `/api/*` on the same origin. `server.js` proxys to: - `https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients` — list clients - `https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/{name}` — get instance info/token - `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connectionState/{name}` — check status The browser never calls Evolution API directly — avoids CORS issues. ### QR Generation Flow 1. User clicks "Connect WhatsApp" on a client card 2. SPA fetches instance token via `/api/token/{name}` 3. Attempts `DELETE /instance/logout/{name}` to clear stale sessions 4. GETs `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connect/{name}` for fresh QR 5. Renders the `base64` data URI in an `` tag inside a modal 6. Starts a 10s `setInterval` to auto-refresh the QR (calls connect again) 7. User scans with WhatsApp Business → instance goes `open` → badge updates ### Instance Status Fetching After rendering client cards, the SPA fetches each instance's `connectionState` via: ``` /api/instance/{name}/status → proxied to Evolution API ``` Status badges: open (green), connecting (amber), close (red). ## Deployment ```bash cd /root/whatsapp-connect # Build docker build -t whatsapp-connect:v2 . # Deploy docker stack rm wa-connect # if updating docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml wa-connect # Check logs docker service logs wa-connect_connect # Verify curl -sI http://localhost:8990/ | head -5 ``` ## DNS Required The target domain must have an A record pointing to `72.61.44.159` in Cloudflare. Without it, LetsEncrypt cert provisioning fails with: ``` DNS problem: NXDOMAIN looking up A for whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com ``` ### CRITICAL: Cloudflare Proxied vs DNS-Only for Cert Provisioning If the Cloudflare DNS record is **proxied (orange cloud)**, Let's Encrypt cannot complete the HTTP-01 challenge because Cloudflare terminates TLS before reaching Traefik. The cert will fail with: ``` Unable to obtain ACME certificate for domains whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com: error: one or more domains had a problem: [whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com] invalid authorization: acme: error: 400 :: DNS problem: NXDOMAIN looking up A for whatsappconnect.paragu-ai.com ``` **Fix:** Set the DNS record to **DNS only (gray cloud)** in Cloudflare dashboard. Wait ~30s for propagation. The cert auto-provisions via Let's Encrypt. After cert is issued (check Traefik logs), switch back to proxied (orange cloud) if you want Cloudflare protection. After the cert is issued, proxied mode works fine because Traefik has a valid cert to serve to Cloudflare's origin connection. ", "file_type": ".md"}

{"success": true, "name": "evolution-whatsapp-ai-agent", "file": "references/qr-auto-refresh-workaround.md", "content": "# QR Auto-Refresh Workaround Evolution API v2.3.7 only supports QR scanning (no pairing code). The QR returned by `GET /instance/connect/{name}` expires in ~60s (count field starts at ~57-65) but the processing delay means by the time you save/write/serve it, only 3-5s remain. ## Problem ```python # This QR will already be count=57+ by the time you process the response "count": 57 # ~3s remaining to scan ``` ## Workaround: Auto-Refreshing HTML Page Serve the QR on a page that auto-refreshes every 15s. Each refresh generates a fresh QR from the Evolution API. ```python import json, base64 from hermes_tools import terminal token = "" r = terminal(f"curl -s 'https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connect/{instance_name}' -H 'apikey: {token}'", timeout=10) data = json.loads(r['output']) b64_uri = data['base64'] # data:image/png;base64,... html = f''' WhatsApp QR - {instance_name} ''' with open('/path/to/public/qr.html', 'w') as f: f.write(html) # Open https://yoursite.com/qr.html on phone # Page refreshes every 15s with a fresh QR ``` ## Steps (Ivan's Setup) 1. Generate QR and create auto-refresh HTML in one shot: ``` cd /root/whatsapp-ai-agent && python3 <<'EOF' import json, base64 from urllib.request import Request, urlopen token = "5C[REDACTED_PHONE]DE-407B-BF96-FFC1D84A79E3" req = Request("https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connect/hermes-whatsapp") req.add_header("apikey", token) data = json.loads(urlopen(req).read()) b64 = data['base64'] html = f'''...''' # as above with open('/root/nexa-paraguay/public/qr-auto.html','w') as f: f.write(html) print("https://nexaparaguay.com/qr-auto.html") EOF ``` 2. Open that URL on your phone browser 3. Open WhatsApp Business → Linked Devices → Link a Device 4. Scan the QR from the browser page 5. Page auto-refreshes every 15s — stay on it until the scan succeeds ## Alternative: Browser Manager UI The Evolution Manager UI at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager` provides a "Get QR Code" button that renders the QR directly in the browser. This avoids the save-to-file delay entirely. Login with the global API key, navigate to the instance, click "Get QR Code", and scan immediately. ## Why Not Pairing Code Evolution API v2.3.7 has NO pairing code endpoints. All of these return 404: - POST /instance/pairingCode/{name} - POST /instance/requestPairingCode/{name} - POST /instance/code/{name} - POST /instance/requestCode/{name} Pairing code is available starting from v2.4+. ", "file_type": ".md"}

{"action": "write_file", "file_path": "references/supabase-key-truncation.md", "name": "evolution-whatsapp-ai-agent", "content": "# Supabase Key Truncation Bug

## Problem
Env files in `/root/` have truncated Supabase keys. The `.env.local` files contain values like:
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbG...SXRg
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=eyJhbG...U06g
```

The `...` is literal — these are not valid JWTs. Some editor or terminal output truncation corrupted the keys during initial setup. The actual full keys are needed from the Supabase dashboard.

## Full Keys for qyvokpribmbrosafntqa

```
# anon key (public, safe to embed in browser)
eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.[REDACTED_TOKEN][REDACTED_TOKEN]0.[REDACTED_TOKEN]

# service_role key (secret — DO NOT embed in browser)
eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.[REDACTED_TOKEN][REDACTED_TOKEN]g1NDk3ODh9.[REDACTED_TOKEN]
```

## Impact
- Any app using the truncated keys gets `Invalid API key` from Supabase
- The WhatsApp Connect SPA couldn't authenticate admins until fixed
- El Viajero production site may be broken if keys are truly truncated (check if Docker build embeds the truncated value or if the build got the real key via a different mechanism)

## Fix for New Deployments
When creating a new app that needs Supabase auth, **never copy keys from truncated `.env.local` files**. Get them fresh from the Supabase dashboard: Project Settings → API → Project API keys.

## Detection
Grep for literal `...` in key files:
```bash
grep -rn '\.\.\.' /root/*/.env* 2>/dev/null | grep 'eyJ'
```
If found, the keys are truncated and must be replaced with the full values.
"}
